name: buntdb
description: buntdb is an embeddable, in-memory key/value database for Go with custom indexing and geospatial support. It persists data to disk via an append-only file for durability, supports ACID transactions with multiple concurrent readers and a single writer, and provides B-tree and R-tree indexes for ordering, iteration, and spatial queries. JSON field indexing is powered by GJSON, enabling indexes on nested document fields. Built-in TTL expiration enables automatic item eviction.
core_strengths: In-memory speed with ACID durability via append-only file persistence
Custom B-tree indexes for ordered iteration and range queries over values
Spatial indexing with R-trees supporting up to 20 dimensions and kNN queries
JSON field indexing via GJSON for querying nested document structures
TTL-based expiration for automatic item eviction without manual cleanup
Zero dependencies beyond the Go standard library and tidwall packages
best_use_cases: Embedded key/value store for Go apps needing in-memory speed with persistence
Local caching layer with TTL-based expiration and durable recovery
Geospatial applications requiring fast spatial intersection and kNN queries
JSON document indexing with queryable nested fields in a lightweight embedded DB
Single-binary Go applications that need ordered iteration over key spaces
remarks: buntdb holds the entire dataset in memory and writes to an append-only file for persistence. This means available RAM limits the dataset size; it is not suitable for datasets exceeding memory capacity. Only one writer is permitted at a time (serialized via internal locking), but multiple readers can operate concurrently. Spatial indexes support up to 20 dimensions. The AOF file is automatically compacted (shrunk) based on configurable thresholds: AutoShrinkPercentage, AutoShrinkMinSize, and AutoShrinkDisabled. For datasets that must exceed RAM, consider alternatives like bbolt or LevelDB.
